nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary or inefficient for paid work
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to draft quick emails to my support team so that I don't have to use my own unpaid time

nodes.label: AI as quick reference for literacy knowledge
nodes.label_negative: AI as inadequate for nuanced literacy tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Usually more of a quick reference tool

nodes.label: AI as quick reference for literacy knowledge
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability in basic literacy tasks
nodes.grounding_span: some models have shown a complete inability to do simple literacy tasks such as counting syllables

nodes.label: AI as tutor for learning and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle actual literacy tutoring decisions myself

nodes.label: AI and child safeguarding concerns
nodes.label_negative: AI missing nuance of child development
nodes.grounding_span: My concern in using AI is that it doesn't capture the nuance and subtleties of working with children

nodes.label: Fair compensation for labor
nodes.grounding_span: I am not okay with working for free

nodes.label: Deep expertise and professional knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: I'm truly curious about the 'why' behind word choices

nodes.label: Student-centered personalized education
nodes.grounding_span: I use my clinical training to essentially observe the 'whole child'

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for email drafting
nodes.grounding_span: I get a lot of satisfaction from the email/message generation that AI provides

nodes.label: Amused frustration at AI literacy failures
nodes.grounding_span: I got a good laugh at how badly the models bungled simple literacy tasks

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in tutoring decisions
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle actual literacy tutoring decisions myself

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: I'll probably use it more in both my professional and personal life

nodes.label: Task-specific compartmentalization
nodes.grounding_span: I do tend to give it specific, practical tasks, primarily administrative ones

nodes.label: Clinical observational reasoning
nodes.grounding_span: I use my clinical training to essentially observe the 'whole child'
